decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the hot spot. We will apply a conservative 1.5x multiplier to the spreader conductivity, moving from 500.0 to 750.0 W/(m*K), which remains within the allowed single-step ratio bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak temperature at the chip center.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity values assume idealized material properties; ensure physical plausibility for the specific application context.
